body:
## Summary
- rename the comments build contract from `GISCUS_*` to canonical `SEPO_COMMENTS_*` variables
- keep Quartz as a fixed-contract consumer with no legacy alias parsing
- update workflow env export/defaults and the discussion ID resolver to emit `SEPO_COMMENTS_*` IDs
- update README docs and local pinned-ID examples for the new names

## Validation
- `npm run check`
- `SEPO_COMMENTS_REPO=self-evolving/repo-discussions SEPO_COMMENTS_REPO_ID=R_kgDOSjgnjQ SEPO_COMMENTS_CATEGORY=General SEPO_COMMENTS_CATEGORY_ID=DIC_kwDOSjgnjc4C9gaF SEPO_PREVIEW_PR=77 SEPO_PREVIEW_BRANCH=agent/demo npm run build`

Stacked on #34.

Learn more about [Vercel for GitHub](https://vercel.link/github-learn-more). | Project | Deployment | Actions | Updated (UTC) | | :--- | :----- | :------ | :------ | | [literature-template](https://vercel.com/codiscover-ai/literature-template) | ![Ready](https://vercel.com/static/status/ready.svg) [Ready](https://vercel.com/codiscover-ai/literature-template/HWq8Ahuzomb2Yk95DcPBtLWuLjt6) | [Preview](https://literature-template-git-stack-sepo-comment-baad17-codiscover-ai.vercel.app), [Comment](https://vercel.live/open-feedback/literature-template-git-stack-sepo-comment-baad17-codiscover-ai.vercel.app?via=pr-comment-feedback-link) | Jun 12, 2026 11:21pm |

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 8 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow/generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | README documents the renamed `SEPO_COMMENTS_*` contract, defaults, strict/off behavior, and local pinned-ID example. [README.md#L142-L214](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/e2fe4c1713c3d9885551157d225030c230a77a6c/README.md#L142-L214) |
| coding_workflow/generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 7/7 | The contract uses distinct env vars for enablement, repo, category, IDs, tabs, app host, and preview metadata instead of overloading a single command/string. [quartz.ts#L87-L205](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/e2fe4c1713c3d9885551157d225030c230a77a6c/quartz.ts#L87-L205) |
| coding_workflow/generic | Name config by capability | pass | 6/6 | Public knobs are named for the Sepo comments capability (`SEPO_COMMENTS_*`) while preview-specific knobs remain under `SEPO_PREVIEW_*`. [README.md#L158-L173](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/e2fe4c1713c3d9885551157d225030c230a77a6c/README.md#L158-L173) |
| coding_workflow/generic | Minimal defaults, native escape hatches | pass | 8/8 | Quartz keeps product defaults fixed for mapping, strict matching, reactions, input position, themes, and language, while documenting only common configuration knobs. [quartz.ts#L183-L205](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/e2fe4c1713c3d9885551157d225030c230a77a6c/quartz.ts#L183-L205), [README.md#L175-L177](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/e2fe4c1713c3d9885551157d225030c230a77a6c/README.md#L175-L177) |
| coding_workflow/generic | Bounded transient retries | pass | 7/7 | The resolver preserves bounded 3-attempt retry behavior only for transient-looking GraphQL failures and warns immediately for deterministic failures. [.github/actions/resolve-discussion-ids/action.yml#L46-L69](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/e2fe4c1713c3d9885551157d225030c230a77a6c/.github/actions/resolve-discussion-ids/action.yml#L46-L69) |
| coding_workflow/generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | The PR renames the existing Quartz consumer, resolver action, and workflow env-prep path rather than adding parallel alias parsing or new resolver logic. [quartz.ts#L94-L214](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/e2fe4c1713c3d9885551157d225030c230a77a6c/quartz.ts#L94-L214), [.github/actions/resolve-discussion-ids/action.yml#L18-L92](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/e2fe4c1713c3d9885551157d225030c230a77a6c/.github/actions/resolve-discussion-ids/action.yml#L18-L92) |
| coding_workflow/generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | The diff is limited to the comments contract rename across five directly related files: Quartz config, two workflows, resolver action, and README. PR files: `.github/actions/resolve-discussion-ids/action.yml`, `.github/workflows/agent-site-preview.yml`, `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`, `README.md`, `quartz.ts`. |
| communication/generic | Write audience-first documentation | pass | 7/7 | The README leads with what site owners configure and how workflows prepare the build env, then gives the variable table and local testing path. [README.md#L142-L214](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/e2fe4c1713c3d9885551157d225030c230a77a6c/README.md#L142-L214) |

## Notes

- PR #35 is stacked on #34, which implements issue #33’s boundary: Quartz consumes a fixed comments/preview env contract while workflows derive provider-specific values. This rename layer preserves that boundary with `SEPO_COMMENTS_*`.
- Validation run on PR head: `npm run check` passed, and the PR-body comments build with pinned `SEPO_COMMENTS_*` IDs passed under Node 22.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric-specific findings.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `high`_

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->
<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis-head: e2fe4c1713c3d9885551157d225030c230a77a6c -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #35 renames the comments build contract from `GISCUS_*` to canonical `SEPO_COMMENTS_*` variables across Quartz config, workflows, resolver action, and docs.
- It is stacked on PR #34 / issue #33, preserving the intended boundary that Quartz only consumes a fixed comments/preview environment contract while workflows prepare defaults and resolve IDs.
- The PR intentionally avoids legacy alias parsing because the underlying contract has not merged yet.
- README examples and local pinned-ID guidance were updated to the new names.

## Review
All current reviewer artifacts support shipping: Codex reported no blocking or warning findings, Rubrics Review passed, and Claude’s notes were INFO-level cosmetic/future-maintenance observations. There are no meaningful reviewer disagreements and no unresolved actionable branch changes.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| No actionable issues | INFO | Current review artifacts and PR state do not show blocking or warning findings. Remaining notes are optional cosmetics or future-maintenance guidance. |

## Progress
- Reviewed all files under `/reviews`: Codex, Rubrics Review, and Claude.
- Inspected current PR conversation, inline comments, and review threads.
- No existing inline review comments or unresolved review threads were present.
- No new inline comments were posted.
- Reviewer validation reports `npm run check` and the pinned `SEPO_COMMENTS_*` build passing.

## Recommended Next Step
`NO_AUTOMATED_ACTION`: No unresolved actionable work remains, so another automated fix-pr pass is not needed.

## Final Verdict
SHIP

## Action Items
- [ ] No required branch-change work remains.

## Rubrics Update

No changes were committed to [`agent/rubrics`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/tree/agent/rubrics) from PR #35.

no rubric changes

I found no trusted, durable PR #35 conversation feedback that warrants a new or updated rubric. The only human PR #35 comment was a `/review` trigger from `lolipopshock`; substantive guidance was bot-authored and already covered by existing rubrics like `name-config-by-capability`, `keep-docs-in-sync`, `minimal-defaults-native-escape-hatches`, and `surgical-changes`.

I left `/Users/shannons/clawd/self-evolving-local-runner/runner-4/_work/_temp/agent-rubrics` unchanged.

title: Rename comments build contract to Sepo
